component.name: Porkill
component.summary: A cyberpunk-themed GUI for monitoring and killing processes by port

description.p: Porkill is a Linux desktop application for visualising every open network port alongside the process that owns it — and killing it with one click.

It reads directly from /proc/net/ with no external tools required, falling back to ss (JSON or legacy mode) or netstat when available. The fastest working method is cached automatically for subsequent refreshes.

ul.li: Real-time port and process monitoring with debounced live filtering
Process grouping — batch-kill all ports owned by a process in one action
Container runtime detection: Docker, Podman, containerd, crun, runc, buildah
Dual-mode kill: graceful SIGTERM or force SIGKILL -9, with PID-reuse safety check
Privilege elevation via pkexec or sudo with a styled confirmation dialog
Neon cyberpunk dark theme with semantic colour coding per protocol and state

screenshot.caption: Main interface — real-time port monitor with process grouping

description.p: Full rewrite from Tkinter to PyQt6. Major improvements:

ul.li: QThreadPool + QRunnable worker architecture for non-blocking UI
Versioned fetch-generation guard prevents stale worker side-effects
Batch tree insertion for smooth rendering at 2 000+ rows
lru_cache port formatter and sort key — zero per-row allocation
Smart tooltip that flips above/below the hovered row
os.scandir-based inode map — 2–3× faster than Path.iterdir
IntEnum column indices for type-safe tree access

description.p: Production hardening: multi-PID group kill, PID reuse mitigation, debounced background filtering, and stable application lifecycle.
